requiredExam.label: Inspect neck for acanthosis

source.source_section: Prediabetes curated endocrine workup: required bedside maneuver
source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

requiredExam.action: Use this maneuver to evaluate insulin resistance or cardiometabolic risk phenotype. Acanthosis supports insulin resistance and prompts metabolic risk counseling.
requiredExam.rationale: Assesses insulin-resistance phenotype and cardiometabolic risk so a positive finding can support diabetes/metabolic screening, counseling intensity, and risk-factor management for Prediabetes.
requiredExam.technique: Inspect the named area directly, comparing sides when relevant and documenting presence, absence, severity, and inability to assess.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: When insulin resistance or metabolic syndrome is suspected
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: insulin resistance or cardiometabolic risk phenotype
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
requiredExam.management_change: Acanthosis supports insulin resistance and prompts metabolic risk counseling.
requiredExam.limitations: Inspect neck for acanthosis is supportive for Prediabetes, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

requiredExam.label: Measure blood pressure

source.source_section: Prediabetes curated endocrine workup: basic bedside data and safety checks
source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

requiredExam.action: Check and document as baseline bedside data for this workup. Hypotension or severe hypertension changes acuity, medication safety, fluid strategy, and escalation.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Prediabetes is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context
requiredExam.management_change: Hypotension or severe hypertension changes acuity, medication safety, fluid strategy, and escalation.
requiredExam.limitations: Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
requiredExam.technique: Measure blood pressure using an appropriately sized cuff after a brief rest period, seated or supine as clinically appropriate.
requiredExam.rationale: Targets hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Prediabetes.

requiredExam.label: Measure heart rate

requiredExam.limitations: Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
requiredExam.technique: Palpate or monitor heart rate for a full interval, noting rate and regularity.
requiredExam.rationale: Screens severity and immediate safety findings that can change triage, monitoring, fluids, or urgent escalation for Prediabetes.

source.source_section: Prediabetes curated endocrine workup: conditional bedside maneuver add-ons
source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

conditionalExam.action: Add when edema, dyspnea, heart failure, kidney disease, albuminuria is present; this evaluates volume overload, venous disease, or medication/renal-cardiac complication. Edema changes cardiometabolic complication framing, medication safety, renal/cardiac evaluation, and follow-up urgency.
conditionalExam.rationale: Targets volume overload, venous disease, or medication/renal-cardiac complication; positive, negative, or unable-to-assess findings guide escalation, diagnostic testing, treatment safety, or specialty planning for Prediabetes.
conditionalExam.technique: Inspect and press over both lower legs or dependent areas for pitting, symmetry, tenderness, and proximal extent.
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: When dyspnea, kidney disease, heart failure, medication effect, or volume overload symptoms are present
conditionalExam.diagnostic_target: volume overload, venous disease, or medication/renal-cardiac complication
conditionalExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
conditionalExam.management_change: Edema changes cardiometabolic complication framing, medication safety, renal/cardiac evaluation, and follow-up urgency.
conditionalExam.limitations: Inspect and press lower leg edema is supportive for Prediabetes, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

conditionalExam.label: Inspect skin for xanthomas or xanthelasma

source.source_section: Prediabetes curated endocrine workup: conditional bedside maneuver add-ons
source.implementation_notes: Guideline-backed endocrine workup content curated into the local knowledge schema; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

conditionalExam.action: Add when severe dyslipidemia, hypertriglyceridemia, pancreatitis, familial lipid disorder, xanthoma is present; this evaluates dyslipidemia phenotype, familial lipid disorder clue, or severe hypertriglyceridemia complication risk. Xanthomas or xanthelasma change lipid disorder severity assessment, familial-risk framing, and urgency of lipid-focused management.
conditionalExam.rationale: Checks complications or precipitating illness that change treatment intensity, prevention, and disposition for Prediabetes.
conditionalExam.technique: Inspect eyelids, extensor surfaces, Achilles tendons, elbows, knees, and pressure areas for xanthelasma, tendon xanthomas, or eruptive xanthomas.
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: When severe dyslipidemia, hypertriglyceridemia, pancreatitis risk, or familial lipid disorder is possible
conditionalExam.diagnostic_target: dyslipidemia phenotype, familial lipid disorder clue, or severe hypertriglyceridemia complication risk
conditionalExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.
conditionalExam.management_change: Xanthomas or xanthelasma change lipid disorder severity assessment, familial-risk framing, and urgency of lipid-focused management.
conditionalExam.limitations: Inspect skin for xanthomas or xanthelasma is supportive for Prediabetes, not diagnostic by itself; reliability can be reduced by pain, positioning, acute illness, cooperation, body habitus, baseline disability, and examiner technique.

reference_values: Fasting plasma glucose: normal <100 mg/dL; prediabetes 100-125; diabetes >=126.
Random plasma glucose >=200 mg/dL with classic symptoms supports diabetes diagnosis.
Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects.
decision_steps: Classify prediabetes with A1c 5.7-6.4%, fasting glucose 100-125 mg/dL, or 2-hour OGTT 140-199 mg/dL after excluding diabetes-range results.
Assess BMI, waist, BP, lipids, fatty liver, sleep apnea, PCOS, prior gestational diabetes, medications, and ASCVD risk.
Repeat diabetes testing on a surveillance interval and sooner if symptoms, steroid exposure, pregnancy, or rising risk appears.
If any confirmatory result reaches diabetes range, switch to the diabetes diagnostic and treatment pathway.
Confirm that Prediabetes is the selected clinical intent, then screen for red flags before routine outpatient testing.
Order the first-line tests in sequence: A1c, fasting plasma glucose, or 2-hour 75-g OGTT; Lipids, ALT if fatty liver risk, BP/BMI/waist.

Interpret numeric results against the workup cutoffs and local assay ranges: A1c: normal <5.7%; prediabetes 5.7-6.4%; diabetes >=6.5%. Fasting plasma glucose: normal <100 mg/dL; prediabetes 100-125; diabetes >=126.

If the initial result is equivocal, discordant with the phenotype, or affected by medication, acute illness, pregnancy, age, sex, or assay interference, repeat or confirm using the next guideline test before committing to long-term therapy.
treatment_options: Offer intensive lifestyle intervention aiming for durable weight loss when appropriate, nutrition quality, physical activity, sleep, and tobacco risk reduction.
Consider metformin for higher-risk adults such as younger age with obesity, prior gestational diabetes, rising A1c, or other strong diabetes-risk features.
Treat BP, lipids, fatty liver risk, sleep apnea, and weight-related comorbidities as part of cardiometabolic risk reduction.
Use shared decision-making for anti-obesity pharmacotherapy or bariatric referral when BMI/comorbidity criteria and patient goals support it.
Treat unstable presentations first using the urgent escalation rule for Prediabetes; draw time-sensitive labs only if doing so does not delay stabilization.
For confirmed Prediabetes, choose therapy based on severity, comorbidities, pregnancy/fertility context, contraindications, patient preference, and local specialty pathway.
Monitor treatment response with the condition-specific clinical endpoint plus the most relevant labs or imaging listed in this workup.
Escalate to endocrinology or the relevant specialty when results are severe, discordant, recurrent, treatment-limiting, or require localization, surgery, genetic testing, or high-risk medication.
